items.prompt: integrates statistical design, probability, and inferential evidence to answer bounded AP-style statistical questions
items.expected_evidence: integrate distribution, association, design, and probability evidence in one bounded inferential argument
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner builds a coherent statistical argument that links model, design, and evidence strength correctly.

items.prompt: explains how chance variation, study design, and probabilistic reasoning determine whether a statistical claim is persuasive
items.expected_evidence: explain how chance variation and study design interact when interpreting evidence strength
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains AP-style statistical evidence as a structured reasoning chain rather than as procedural output.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to inferential_overreach_from_weak_design.
items.expected_evidence: critique AP-style statistical claims for misuse of probability, bias, or inferential overreach
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies where a claim exceeded what the design, model, or evidence could support.

items.prompt: interprets a less-familiar AP-style statistics scenario and identifies the most defensible conclusion under uncertainty
items.expected_evidence: compare competing statistical explanations and identify the strongest supported conclusion
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves coherent probabilistic and inferential reasoning in a varied AP-style context.
